title: [InstCombine] Avoid infinite loop when negating phi nodes

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-n32:64-S128"

define i32 @diamond_cfg_phi_negation(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp sgt i32 %a, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %if.then, label %if.else

if.then:
  %neg1 = sub i32 0, %a
  br label %merge

if.else:
  %neg2 = sub i32 0, %b
  br label %merge

merge:
  %phi = phi i32 [ %neg1, %if.then ], [ %neg2, %if.else ]
  ret i32 %phi
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-n32:64-S128"

define i32 @diamond_cfg_phi_negation(i32 %a, i32 %b) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp sgt i32 %a, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %if.then, label %if.else

if.then:
  br label %merge

if.else:
  br label %merge

merge:
  %phi.neg = phi i32 [ %a, %if.then ], [ %b, %if.else ]
  %result = sub i32 0, %phi.neg
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Tests negation sinking through PHI in a diamond CFG without any loops. The summary focuses on preventing infinite recursion in cyclic CFGs, but this tests the optimization in acyclic control flow where dominance checks pass normally.

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-n32:64-S128"

define i32 @chained_phi_negation(i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c) {
entry:
  %cmp1 = icmp sgt i32 %a, 0
  br i1 %cmp1, label %if.then1, label %if.else1

if.then1:
  %neg1 = sub i32 0, %a
  br label %merge1

if.else1:
  %neg2 = sub i32 0, %b
  br label %merge1

merge1:
  %phi1 = phi i32 [ %neg1, %if.then1 ], [ %neg2, %if.else1 ]
  %cmp2 = icmp sgt i32 %phi1, 0
  br i1 %cmp2, label %if.then2, label %if.else2

if.then2:
  %neg3 = sub i32 0, %phi1
  br label %merge2

if.else2:
  %neg4 = sub i32 0, %c
  br label %merge2

merge2:
  %phi2 = phi i32 [ %neg3, %if.then2 ], [ %neg4, %if.else2 ]
  ret i32 %phi2
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-n32:64-S128"

define i32 @chained_phi_negation(i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c) {
entry:
  %cmp1 = icmp sgt i32 %a, 0
  br i1 %cmp1, label %if.then1, label %if.else1

if.then1:
  br label %merge1

if.else1:
  br label %merge1

merge1:
  %phi1.neg = phi i32 [ %a, %if.then1 ], [ %b, %if.else1 ]
  %phi1 = sub i32 0, %phi1.neg
  %cmp2 = icmp sgt i32 %phi1, 0
  br i1 %cmp2, label %if.then2, label %if.else2

if.then2:
  br label %merge2

if.else2:
  br label %merge2

merge2:
  %phi2.neg = phi i32 [ %phi1, %if.then2 ], [ %c, %if.else2 ]
  %result = sub i32 0, %phi2.neg
  ret i32 %result
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Tests multiple PHI nodes in sequence where negation sinking applies at multiple levels. The summary describes single PHI node handling, but doesn't cover cascading optimizations through chains of PHI nodes in acyclic regions.

define i32 @chained_phi_negation(i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c) {
entry:

if.then1:

merge1:

  %cmp2 = icmp sgt i32 %phi1, 0
  br i1 %cmp2, label %if.then2, label %if.else2

if.then2:
  %neg3 = sub i32 0, %phi1
  br label %merge2

if.else2:
  %neg4 = sub i32 0, %c
  br label %merge2

merge2:
  %phi2 = phi i32 [ %neg3, %if.then2 ], [ %neg4, %if.else2 ]

}

define i32 @chained_phi_negation(i32 %a, i32 %b, i32 %c) {
entry:
  %cmp1 = icmp sgt i32 %a, 0
  br i1 %cmp1, label %if.then1, label %if.else1

if.then1:                                         ; preds = %entry

if.else1:                                         ; preds = %entry

merge1:                                           ; preds = %if.else1, %if.then1
  %a.pn = phi i32 [ %a, %if.then1 ], [ %b, %if.else1 ]
  %cmp2 = icmp ugt i32 %a.pn, -2147483648
  br i1 %cmp2, label %if.then2, label %if.else2

if.then2:                                         ; preds = %merge1
  br label %merge2

if.else2:                                         ; preds = %merge1
  %neg4 = sub i32 0, %c
  br label %merge2

merge2:                                           ; preds = %if.else2, %if.then2
  %phi2 = phi i32 [ %a.pn, %if.then2 ], [ %neg4, %if.else2 ]
  ret i32 %phi2
}

tests.source_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-n32:64-S128"

define i32 @mixed_dominance_phi(i32 %x) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:
  %phi = phi i32 [ %x, %entry ], [ %next, %loop ]
  %neg = sub i32 0, %phi
  %next = add i32 %neg, 1
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %next, 100
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop, label %exit

exit:
  ret i32 %next
}
tests.expect_optimized_program:
target datalayout = "e-m:e-p:64:64-i64:64-i128:128-n32:64-S128"

define i32 @mixed_dominance_phi(i32 %x) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:
  %phi = phi i32 [ %x, %entry ], [ %next, %loop ]
  %neg = sub i32 0, %phi
  %next = add i32 %neg, 1
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %next, 100
  br i1 %cmp, label %loop, label %exit

exit:
  ret i32 %next
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Tests PHI with mixed dominance - one incoming value from non-dominated block (entry) and one from dominated block (loop backedge). The summary only mentions self-referencing PHI, not mixed predecessor scenarios.

  %next = add i32 %neg, 1
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %next, 100

exit:
